nativeRange.habitat: Rocky intertidal zones and shallow coral reef flats in high-energy, wave-swept environments; tends to form dense colonies on exposed surfaces
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical coral reef intertidal and shallow subtidal

avoidWith: aggressive herbivorous fish that may graze it heavily (tangs, surgeonfish, sea urchins)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing (only trade source stocking this species).

summary: A rare Indo-Pacific brown alga with distinctive turbinate (top-shaped) saucer leaflets on a central thallus. Very slow-growing and rarely available in the trade; generally advised for experienced reef keepers.
careNotes:
Turbinaria ornata is a brown macroalga (class Phaeophyceae) in family Sargassaceae, order Fucales — the same family as Sargassum. It is commonly called Ornate Turbanweed or, in Hawaiian tradition, Limu Kahili. The species features small, toothed, turbinate (cone- or top-shaped) bladder-like leaflets arranged on a central stalk, typically ranging from yellow-brown to dark brown.

IMPORTANT TRADE-NAME DISAMBIGUATION: The genus name "Turbinaria" is shared by two completely unrelated organisms: Turbinaria ornata, a brown macroalga (division Ochrophyta, order Fucales) described here; and Turbinaria spp. (cup corals / scroll corals), which are stony corals (order Scleractinia, family Dendrophylliidae) also known as disc coral, vase coral, or pagoda coral. These are entirely different life forms — one is a photosynthetic alga, the other is a colonial cnidarian animal. When searching for or purchasing either organism, confirming the full scientific name and the seller's category (alga vs. coral) is strongly advised.

Mosaic Macros describes this species as a very slow grower that generally does best in turbulent, high-flow conditions, consistent with its wild habitat on wave-swept reef flats. Care requirements are reportedly similar to Sargassum, suggesting moderate-to-medium lighting is adequate. The species is considered appropriate for seasoned aquarists, reflecting its slow growth and sensitivity compared to fast-growing refugium macros.

In the wild, T. ornata is widely distributed across the Indo-Pacific and central Pacific, where it can become locally dominant on rocky intertidal reef zones. It disperses by releasing buoyant older fronds that travel on surface currents. Pharmaceutical research has identified bioactive compounds (antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic properties) in wild-collected material.

Mosaic Macros is the sole known hobby trade source for this species; it is marked as sold out and very rarely available. Standard reef water parameters (SG 1.023–1.026, 23–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4) are appropriate. Propagation in captivity is by fragmentation of the thallus. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is typically by fragmentation: detaching a portion and anchoring it to rockwork. Sexual reproduction involves alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations, as is typical in brown algae, but this has not been documented as a meaningful propagation path in hobby aquariums.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source. Confirmed common name Ornate Turbanweed, scientific name Turbinaria ornata, very slow growth rate, turbulent-water preference, intermediate/advanced care level, and compatibility with reef invertebrates.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Turbinaria ornata
additional.notes: Confirmed family Sargassaceae, order Fucales, class Phaeophyceae; native range Indo-Pacific/central Pacific; adult size 2–20 cm; intertidal reef habitat; Hawaiian name Limu Kahili. Used for taxonomy, native range, and size data.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Turbinaria (coral)
additional.notes: Consulted to document trade-name collision: Turbinaria corals are order Scleractinia, family Dendrophylliidae — entirely unrelated to the brown alga Turbinaria ornata.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
